title: Arrow Function Conventions
summary: Declare functions as const arrow functions for a consistent module style.
purpose: Use one predictable function declaration style across components, hooks, helpers, handlers, mappers, and services.
rule: Declare named behavior with const arrow functions. Public module APIs should export the const directly, while private helpers should stay local to the file.
whenToUse: Creating event handlers, mappers, services, utilities, or test helpers.
Exporting a function from a module.
whenNotToUse: Do not rewrite third-party, generated, or copied reference code unless it becomes owned company code.
Do not use an inline arrow when a named const would make stack traces and review comments clearer.

recommended.title: Use const arrow functions

avoid.title: Avoid function declarations

why: A single function style makes files easier to scan, keeps exports consistent, and avoids mixing component, hook, helper, and service declarations in different styles.
checklist: No new function xxx declarations were added.
Exported functions use export const xxx = () => {.
Inline callbacks are used only when they stay small and local.
The function name still communicates the responsibility clearly.
